name: Soup De Poisson
recipeIngredient: 2 Pounds fish bones (including gills and eyes)
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
1 Medium onion, skin on, cut into chard
1 small carrot, peeled and thinly sliced
1 cup chopped fresh tomatoes or canned tomatoes
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F.
2. Spread fish bones, lobster bodies, and fish scraps on sheet pans. Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt and pepper. Toss well.
3. Roast for 30 minutes or until all the fish parts are golden brown.
4. Meanwhile, heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a large stock pot. Add onions, leeks, celery, carrots, and garlic. Cook over high heat, stirring occasionally, until vegetables begin to brown.
5. Add all the fish and white wine. Reduce by one third.
6. Add remaining ingredients to the stock pot. Add enough water to just barely cover the bones. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer and cook 40 minutes.
7. Puree the soup in batches using a food processor.
8. Strain through a coarse chinois.
9. Taste for seasoning. If needed, return to stove and reduce.
10. Slice the baguette into 1/4-inch slices and toast until golden brown.
11. Mix aioli with cayenne pepper and paprika. It should be spicy.
12. To serve, pour soup into hot bowls and serve with croutons, rouille, grated Gruyere cheese, and chopped parsley on the side.
